requests.description: Ethereum RLUSD contract supply data to reconcile the total. RLUSD velocity metrics per chain.
requests.disposition_reasoning: Ethereum RLUSD supply data directly resolves T-9 (chain distribution) and informs T-4 (RLUSD kill switch). The ~$1.1B off-XRPL delta is the single largest blind spot in the RLUSD assessment. This data is publicly available on Ethereum and would immediately clarify whether RLUSD's XRPL share is growing, stable, or declining.

requests.description: Specific deal terms regarding stablecoin yield treatment for banks vs. non-bank issuers.
requests.disposition_reasoning: Stablecoin yield deal terms are important but the assessment can proceed without them this cycle. The deal's directional impact on RLUSD is ambiguous regardless — knowing the terms changes the competitive landscape assessment but does not change the observable RLUSD contraction or kill switch trajectory.

requests.description: Technical infrastructure details for Nasdaq's tokenized securities trading system.
requests.disposition_reasoning: Nasdaq's infrastructure choice directly informs T-7 (competitive displacement) and CI-5. If Nasdaq is building on Ethereum or proprietary infrastructure, the competitive displacement assessment strengthens materially. If chain-agnostic or XRPL-compatible, the assessment changes. This is one of the highest-impact unknowns in the competitive landscape.

requests.description: What is the RLUSD supply on the Ethereum contract, and how has it changed over the past 30 days?
requests.preferred_vendor: Ethereum blockchain — RLUSD ERC-20 contract supply data via Etherscan or similar
requests._l3_impact_on_analysis: Would resolve the ~$1.1B RLUSD chain distribution question. If Ethereum RLUSD is growing while XRPL RLUSD is flat/declining, it confirms that RLUSD success is not validating XRPL. If Ethereum RLUSD is also declining, it indicates broader RLUSD weakness regardless of chain.
requests._l3_estimated_value: High — resolves a blind spot in the settlement infrastructure thesis
requests.disposition_reasoning: Duplicate of ACQ-L2-001. Same rationale — Ethereum RLUSD supply resolves T-9. Consolidate with L2 request.

requests.description: What blockchain infrastructure is Nasdaq using for its SEC-approved tokenized securities trading?
requests.preferred_vendor: Nasdaq technical documentation, SEC filing details, Nasdaq partner announcements
requests._l3_impact_on_analysis: Would resolve the competitive displacement vs. potential interoperability question for CI-5. If Ethereum or proprietary: confirms competitive displacement. If chain-agnostic or XRPL-compatible: changes the competitive assessment significantly.
requests._l3_estimated_value: High — determines whether the biggest tokenization development of the cycle is thesis-positive or thesis-negative
requests.disposition_reasoning: Duplicate of ACQ-L2-003. Same rationale — Nasdaq infrastructure choice resolves competitive displacement question. Consolidate with L2 request.

requests.description: Have the 3 non-reporting XRP ETF funds formally closed, or are they still operational but not reporting to Coinglass?
requests.preferred_vendor: SEC EDGAR filings for XRP ETF funds, fund company websites, or direct fund family disclosures
requests._l3_impact_on_analysis: Fund closures vs. reporting gaps have very different implications. Closures indicate structural product failure. Reporting gaps might just indicate data source limitations. This affects the kill switch assessment for ETF AUM.
requests._l3_estimated_value: Medium — clarifies severity of ETF structural deterioration
requests.disposition_reasoning: ETF fund closure vs. reporting gap directly informs T-5 (ETF kill switch). Fund closures would indicate structural product failure; reporting gaps would be a data artifact. This clarification is available through SEC EDGAR and changes the kill switch severity assessment.

requests.description: What is the current USD/JPY rate as of March 23, 2026?
requests.preferred_vendor: FX market data (any live FX feed)
requests._l3_impact_on_analysis: Current data is 3 days stale (March 20). Given the Hormuz escalation on March 22-23, USD/JPY may have crossed the 160 critical threshold. This would upgrade the compound stress assessment from CRITICAL to near-EMERGENCY and change the kill switch proximity assessment.
requests._l3_estimated_value: High — directly determines compound stress classification
requests.disposition_reasoning: Current USD/JPY is the highest-urgency data need. The 3-day lag during a fast-moving crisis creates a temporal distortion (LZ-MR-003) that directly affects compound stress classification. USD/JPY at or past 160 would change the stress assessment from CRITICAL to near-EMERGENCY. This should be acquired immediately.

requests.description: What are the specific terms of the Senate-White House stablecoin yield deal?
requests.preferred_vendor: Congressional records, press briefings, legislative text if available
requests._l3_impact_on_analysis: Terms determine whether the deal favors bank-issued stablecoins (negative for RLUSD — consistent with Dimon push and ECB warning) or creates a level playing field. This affects the competitive landscape for kill switch #2 (RLUSD).
requests._l3_estimated_value: Medium — affects stablecoin competitive landscape assessment
requests.disposition_reasoning: Same rationale as ACQ-L2-002. Deal terms are informative but do not change the observable RLUSD trajectory or immediate kill switch assessment. Defer to next cycle.

requests.description: Actual deal terms: does it enable or constrain yield-bearing stablecoins? Does it apply to bank-issued stablecoins only or all issuers? How does it affect non-bank stablecoin issuers like Ripple/RLUSD?
requests.disposition_reasoning: Stablecoin yield deal terms are important for CI-3-E and RLUSD competitive assessment, but the deal terms may not yet be publicly available (legislative process in progress). Assessment can proceed without this data this cycle — the signal is already scored as directionally unknown with low confidence. Will re-evaluate when legislative text is published.

requests.description: Technical architecture of XRPL PermissionedDEX: are permissioned transactions visible in public DEX volume queries? Or do they occur on credential-gated ledger subsets invisible to public queries?
requests.disposition_reasoning: This is the single most decision-relevant knowledge gap in the assessment. Whether PermDEX transactions are visible in public DEX data fundamentally changes the interpretation of CI-2-C and tension T-8. If PermDEX is invisible, we cannot score non-adoption; if visible, 31 days of minimal volume confirms non-adoption. Resolution directly impacts three kill switches and CI-2 convergence assessment.

requests.description: RLUSD supply on Ethereum chain. Ethereum contract address for RLUSD to query on-chain supply directly.
requests.disposition_reasoning: RLUSD Ethereum supply data resolves the chain-distribution question that affects T-9 and the structural validity of using total RLUSD market cap as a kill switch proxy. If RLUSD is predominantly Ethereum-based, the $5B kill switch needs to be bifurcated between XRPL-specific and total RLUSD. High thesis relevance.

requests.description: Is PermissionedDEX institutional activity architecturally visible in public XRPL DEX volume data, or does it occur on a credential-gated layer invisible to public queries?
requests.preferred_vendor: XRPL technical documentation, Ripple developer documentation, or direct query to PermDEX-enabled XRPL node
requests._l3_impact_on_analysis: If PermDEX is invisible to public queries: the $2.55M daily volume is irrelevant to institutional adoption assessment and CI-2-C must be reclassified as NOT_OBSERVABLE. If PermDEX is visible: 31 days of $2.55M confirms non-adoption and CI-2-C deterioration is confirmed. This is the single most decision-relevant knowledge gap.
requests._l3_estimated_value: Critical — changes classification of entire CI-2-C component
requests.disposition_reasoning: Duplicate of ACQ-L2-002 — same question from different layer. Consolidate with L2 request. Approval reasoning identical: most decision-relevant knowledge gap.

requests.description: What is the RLUSD supply on Ethereum? What percentage of total RLUSD exists on XRPL vs Ethereum?
requests.preferred_vendor: Ethereum blockchain query for RLUSD contract address (0x8292Bb45bf1Ee4d140127049757C2C3c532F33ba or similar), Etherscan token tracker
requests._l3_impact_on_analysis: If >70% of RLUSD is on Ethereum: the XRPL settlement layer thesis is structurally challenged — RLUSD growth would validate Ripple's stablecoin business but NOT the XRPL-specific settlement thesis. Kill switch evaluation would need modification. If >50% on XRPL: thesis alignment confirmed, on-chain activity should be detectable through XRPL metrics.
requests._l3_estimated_value: High — resolves a structural thesis question
requests.disposition_reasoning: Duplicate of ACQ-L2-003 — same question from different layer. Consolidate with L2 request. Approval reasoning identical.

requests.description: What are the terms of the Senate-White House stablecoin yield deal? Does it enable or constrain yield-bearing stablecoins for non-bank issuers?
requests.preferred_vendor: Congressional Record, Senate Banking Committee press releases, draft legislation text
requests._l3_impact_on_analysis: If deal enables non-bank stablecoin yield: RLUSD competitive position improved, CI-3 strengthened. If deal constrains to bank-only: RLUSD faces additional competitive headwind from bank-issued stablecoins with yield advantages. Direction of this signal is currently unknowable.
requests._l3_estimated_value: Moderate — affects CI-3-E and RLUSD competitive assessment
requests.disposition_reasoning: Duplicate of ACQ-L2-001 — same question from different layer. Deferred for same reasoning: legislative text may not be available yet.

requests.description: What is the current USD/JPY rate post-Hormuz escalation? The latest reading (158.77) is from March 20, before the Hormuz threat broke.
requests.preferred_vendor: Real-time forex data (frankfurter API, Bloomberg, Reuters)
requests._l3_impact_on_analysis: If USD/JPY has broken 160: compound stress matrix transitions from CRITICAL to approaching EMERGENCY per defined thresholds. If USD/JPY has weakened to 155-157: the oil shock feedback loop to yen is slower than expected, giving BOJ more time. The 3-day staleness during a period of acute geopolitical escalation creates significant temporal distortion per LZ-MR-003.
requests._l3_estimated_value: High — directly affects compound stress level classification
requests.disposition_reasoning: USD/JPY staleness during acute geopolitical escalation is a critical gap. The 3-day-old reading predates the Hormuz threat. Current USD/JPY directly affects compound stress level classification — if above 160, we may already be at EMERGENCY threshold proximity. IMMEDIATE urgency is justified. This data is freely available and high impact.
